query: Twitter X profile VPN usage indicator transparency rollout

mako.raw:
{"query":"Twitter X profile VPN usage indicator transparency rollout","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1522,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://usa.inquirer.net/184189/x-set-to-display-vpn-alerts-as-part-of-account-transparency-push","title":"X prepares to flag VPN usage on profiles","content":"According to an early preview of the update, X will “show a warning on your profile page if it finds that you’re connected via a proxy or VPN service.” The update notes that this may alter the country or region displayed on your account.
At the same time, the feature could reveal coordinated or anonymous accounts that rely heavily on VPNs to obscure their origins.
This development follows an announcement from Head of Product Nikita Bier, who said last month that X was “experimenting with displaying new information on profiles, including which country an account is based, among other details.”"},{"url":"https://www.socialmediatoday.com/news/x-formerly-twitter-account-info-indicate-using-vpn-location/805718/","title":"X Will Soon Indicate if a Profile Is Using a VPN | Social Media Today","content":"X has indicated that these new profile elements will be rolling out very soon, while it’s also working on another new data point that could be equally valuable in this respect: An indicator of whether an account is using a VPN to hide its true location.
Now that the deal is done, we watch as the Musk-Twitter era begins.
© 2025 TechTarget, Inc. or its subsidiaries."},{"url":"https://www.varindia.com/news/x-to-flag-vpn-use-for-location-transparency","title":"X to Flag VPN Use for Location Transparency","content":"The new feature is expected to display a warning message on a user's profile, such as "Country/region may not be accurate," if the platform detects the use of a VPN or proxy service.
This indicator will be accessible through a new "About this profile" section.
Organizations argue that VPNs are essential tools used by activists, journalists, and individuals in repressive regimes to protect their safety and privacy, warning that the new indicator could potentially expose them."},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2025/11/21/x-begins-rolling-out-the-about-this-account-feature-to-users-profiles/","title":"X begins rolling out the 'About this account' feature to users' profiles | TechCrunch","content":"Elon Musk’s X has begun rolling out a new feature for user profiles that will display information about the account, including where it’s based, how many times the account has changed its username, the account’s original join date, and how the user downloaded the X app.
X’s plans for the feature were first announced in October, when X’s head of product Nikita Bier said the company would experiment with displaying this information on profiles, starting with his own account and those of X employees.
X will show a warning on your account if you try to use a VPN to hide where your account is from when the upcoming "About Your Account" feature launches.
Sarah has worked as a reporter for TechCrunch since August 2011.
You can contact or verify outreach from Sarah by emailing sarahp@techcrunch.com or via encrypted message at sarahperez.01 on Signal."},{"url":"https://www.thenews.com.pk/latest/1381953-x-to-introduce-feature-to-flag-users-hiding-their-location-with-vpns","title":"X to introduce feature to flag users hiding their location with VPNs","content":"The social media platform X is planning to roll out a new feature that will provide more transparency about a user’s account location by indicating whether someone is using a Virtual Private Network (VPN).
The disclosure of VPN usage originates from an upcoming “About Your Account” feature X plans to introduce.
It is quite unsure whether displaying the use of a VPN is currently being tested as part of this feature.
In this connection, representatives from VPN companies Surfshark and NordVPN expressed concern about revealing VPN users could put people with valid privacy issues at risk."}]}

Elon Musk's X is reportedly preparing to introduce new transparency features that will display additional information on user profiles, a move that could make it easier to identify troll accounts and coordinated influence operations on the platform. Users will be able to access this information by tapping a new ‘About this profile’ section. The platform is also reportedly working on an indicator that will show whether an account is using a VPN to hide its real location. Reports note that reduced moderation measures on X may have made the platform more vulnerable to such campaigns, increasing the need for tools that provide clarity about who is behind certain posts. The ‘About this profile’ section is expected to launch on X in the near future.
